questions.question: बाररश आने पर धरती कैसी लगती ह?

facts.fact: Nagada is a traditional Indian percussion instrument made from metal or clay.
Rainbow is formed when sunlight passes through water droplets in the air, creating a colorful arc.
The first drop of rain fell on the earth on the first day of rain, marking the beginning of the rainy season.
Nagada's sound is created by beating it with sticks, producing a deep resonant tone.
Rain has several effects on the environment, including replenishing water sources and supporting plant growth.
The green leafy bud of the plant is a symbol of new life and growth.
Nagada is used in traditional Indian music, particularly during festivals and celebrations.
Rainbow is a symbol of hope and beauty, reminding us of the wonders of nature.
The first day of rain is an important event, marking the beginning of the rainy season.
Nagada's drumhead can be made from various materials, including metal, clay, or animal skin.
